aws-glue:position-deletes:v2.notes: Supported via Spark on Glue 3.0+ (Iceberg 0.13.1+); Glue 4.0+ (Iceberg 1.0.0) recommended for production
caveats: Glue 3.0 (Iceberg 0.13.1) requires DynamoDB lock manager for atomic transactions

aws-glue:position-deletes:v3.notes: V3 replaces positional deletes with deletion vectors (Puffin files); requires Glue 5.0+ (Iceberg 1.7.1+), Glue 5.1 (Iceberg 1.10.0) recommended
caveats: Requires Glue 5.0+ for V3 format support
Glue 5.1 (Iceberg 1.10.0) recommended for full deletion vector support

aws-glue:equality-deletes:v2.notes: Supported via Spark on Glue 3.0+; Glue 4.0+ recommended

aws-glue:equality-deletes:v3.notes: V3 replaces equality deletes with deletion vectors; requires Glue 5.0+, Glue 5.1 recommended
caveats: Requires Glue 5.0+ for V3 format support
V3 deletion vectors supersede V2 equality delete files

aws-glue:merge-on-read:v2.notes: Merge-on-read supported via Spark on Glue 3.0+

aws-glue:merge-on-read:v3.notes: Merge-on-read with V3 deletion vectors; requires Glue 5.0+, Glue 5.1 recommended for best performance
caveats: Requires Glue 5.0+ for V3 format support

aws-glue:copy-on-write:v2.notes: Copy-on-write supported via Spark on Glue 3.0+

aws-glue:copy-on-write:v3.notes: Copy-on-write supported via Spark on Glue 5.0+
caveats: Requires Glue 5.0+ for V3 format support

aws-glue:schema-evolution:v2.notes: Full schema evolution (add, drop, rename, reorder columns) via Spark SQL on Glue 3.0+; ALTER TABLE RENAME not available on Glue 3.0
caveats: ALTER TABLE ... RENAME TO not available on Glue 3.0 (Iceberg 0.13.1)

aws-glue:schema-evolution:v3.notes: Full schema evolution supported via Spark on Glue 5.0+
caveats: Requires Glue 5.0+ for V3 format support

aws-glue:column-default-values:v2.notes: Column default values are a V3 feature, not available in format version 2

aws-glue:column-default-values:v3.notes: Supported via Spark on Glue 5.0+ (Iceberg 1.7.1+); requires format-version=3
caveats: Requires Glue 5.0+ with V3 format tables

aws-glue:nanosecond-timestamps:v2.notes: Nanosecond timestamps are a V3 feature, not available in format version 2

aws-glue:nanosecond-timestamps:v3.notes: Supported via Spark on Glue 5.0+ (Iceberg 1.7.1+); requires format-version=3
caveats: Requires Glue 5.0+ with V3 format tables

aws-glue:hidden-partitioning:v2.notes: Supports identity, bucket, truncate, year, month, day, hour partition transforms via Spark on Glue 3.0+

aws-glue:hidden-partitioning:v3.notes: Full hidden partitioning supported via Spark on Glue 5.0+
caveats: Requires Glue 5.0+ for V3 format support

aws-glue:partition-evolution:v2.notes: Partition evolution supported via Spark SQL ALTER TABLE on Glue 3.0+

aws-glue:partition-evolution:v3.notes: Partition evolution supported via Spark on Glue 5.0+
caveats: Requires Glue 5.0+ for V3 format support

aws-glue:multi-arg-transforms:v2.notes: Multi-argument transforms are a V3 feature, not available in format version 2

aws-glue:multi-arg-transforms:v3.notes: Supported via Spark on Glue 5.0+ (Iceberg 1.7.1+); requires format-version=3
caveats: Requires Glue 5.0+ with V3 format tables

aws-glue:time-travel:v2.notes: Query by snapshot ID or timestamp via Spark SQL on Glue 3.0+

aws-glue:time-travel:v3.notes: Time travel supported via Spark on Glue 5.0+; spans V2 and V3 snapshots after upgrade
caveats: Requires Glue 5.0+ for V3 format support

aws-glue:table-maintenance:v2.notes: Spark procedures (rewrite_data_files, expire_snapshots, remove_orphan_files) available on Glue 4.0+; AWS Glue Data Catalog provides automatic compaction, snapshot expiry, and orphan file removal
caveats: Glue 3.0 (Iceberg 0.13.1) has limited maintenance procedures
Automatic optimization requires Glue Data Catalog configuration

links.label: Working with Iceberg in AWS Glue (prescriptive guidance)

aws-glue:table-maintenance:v3.notes: Full table maintenance for V3 tables; automatic optimization via Glue Data Catalog compacts deletion vector files and removes old V2 delete files after upgrade
caveats: Requires Glue 5.0+ for V3 format support
First compaction after V2→V3 upgrade removes old positional/equality delete files

aws-glue:branching-tagging:v2.notes: Supported via Glue ETL. Glue: Branching and tagging require Iceberg ≥1.2.0; only available natively on Glue 5.0+ (Iceberg 1.7.1) or via custom JARs on Glue 4.0
caveats: Not available on Glue 3.0 or Glue 4.0 with native Iceberg (0.13.1 / 1.0.0)
Requires Glue 5.0+ or custom Iceberg JARs ≥1.2.0

aws-glue:branching-tagging:v3.notes: Supported via Glue ETL. Glue: Branching and tagging supported on Glue 5.0+ (Iceberg 1.7.1+) with V3 tables
caveats: Requires Glue 5.0+ for V3 format support

aws-glue:read-support:v2.notes: Full read support via Spark on Glue 3.0+; supports GlueContext DynamicFrame and Spark DataFrame APIs

aws-glue:read-support:v3.notes: Full read support for V3 tables on Glue 5.0+; handles deletion vectors transparently
caveats: Requires Glue 5.0+ for V3 format support

aws-glue:write-insert:v2.notes: INSERT INTO, CTAS, and DataFrame append supported via Spark and GlueContext APIs on Glue 3.0+

aws-glue:write-insert:v3.notes: INSERT INTO supported on Glue 5.0+ with V3 format tables
caveats: Requires Glue 5.0+ for V3 format support

aws-glue:write-merge-update-delete:v2.notes: MERGE, UPDATE, DELETE supported via Spark SQL with IcebergSparkSessionExtensions on Glue 3.0+; GlueContext native API supports append/overwrite only
caveats: Requires IcebergSparkSessionExtensions for UPDATE/DELETE/MERGE
GlueContext write_data_frame API limited to append and overwrite modes

aws-glue:write-merge-update-delete:v3.notes: MERGE, UPDATE, DELETE supported via Spark SQL on Glue 5.0+; V3 deletion vectors improve write performance for updates and deletes
caveats: Requires Glue 5.0+ for V3 format support
Requires IcebergSparkSessionExtensions for UPDATE/DELETE/MERGE

aws-glue:catalog-integration:v2.notes: Native AWS Glue Data Catalog integration as Iceberg catalog; also supports Iceberg REST Catalog API

links.label: Working with Iceberg in AWS Glue (prescriptive guidance)

aws-glue:catalog-integration:v3.notes: Native AWS Glue Data Catalog integration with V3 support; Iceberg REST Catalog API supported
caveats: Requires Glue 5.0+ for V3 format support

aws-glue:hive-metastore:v2.notes: Native Hive Metastore support on EMR. Glue: Can connect to external Hive Metastore via custom Spark catalog configuration; Glue Data Catalog is the default and recommended catalog
caveats: Requires custom Spark configuration
Not the default catalog; Glue Data Catalog is preferred

aws-glue:hive-metastore:v3.notes: Native Hive Metastore support on EMR. Glue: Can connect to external Hive Metastore via custom Spark configuration on Glue 5.0+
caveats: Requires custom Spark configuration
Not the default catalog; Glue Data Catalog is preferred

aws-glue:aws-glue-catalog:v2.notes: Native default catalog for AWS Glue; pre-configured as glue_catalog with GlueCatalog implementation

links.label: Working with Iceberg in AWS Glue (prescriptive guidance)

aws-glue:aws-glue-catalog:v3.notes: Native default catalog for AWS Glue with V3 table support; automatic table optimization available
caveats: Requires Glue 5.0+ for V3 format support

aws-glue:rest-catalog:v2.notes: AWS Glue Data Catalog supports the Iceberg REST Catalog API, enabling cross-engine interoperability

aws-glue:rest-catalog:v3.notes: AWS Glue Data Catalog REST Catalog API supports V3 tables

aws-glue:nessie:v2.notes: Nessie catalog supported via Glue ETL. Glue: Can connect to Nessie via custom Spark catalog configuration on Glue; not natively integrated
caveats: Requires custom Spark configuration and Nessie endpoint
Nessie JARs must be provided via --extra-jars

aws-glue:nessie:v3.notes: Nessie catalog supported via Glue ETL. Glue: Can connect to Nessie via custom Spark configuration on Glue 5.0+
caveats: Requires custom Spark configuration, Nessie endpoint, and extra JARs

aws-glue:polaris:v2.notes: Polaris supported via REST Catalog on EMR. Glue: Can connect to Polaris via REST Catalog configuration on Glue
caveats: Requires custom Spark REST Catalog configuration

aws-glue:polaris:v3.notes: Polaris supported via REST Catalog on EMR. Glue: Can connect to Polaris via REST Catalog configuration on Glue 5.0+
caveats: Requires custom Spark REST Catalog configuration

aws-glue:unity-catalog:v2.notes: Can connect to Unity Catalog via REST Catalog API on EMR. Glue: AWS Glue does not natively support Databricks Unity Catalog
caveats: Requires Databricks REST Catalog endpoint configuration

aws-glue:unity-catalog:v3.notes: Can connect to Unity Catalog via REST Catalog API on EMR. Glue: AWS Glue does not natively support Databricks Unity Catalog
caveats: Requires Databricks REST Catalog endpoint configuration

aws-glue:hadoop-catalog:v2.notes: Hadoop catalog supported via Glue ETL. Glue: Can use Hadoop catalog via custom Spark configuration on Glue; not recommended for production
caveats: Requires custom Spark configuration
No atomic rename guarantee on S3; Glue Data Catalog recommended instead

aws-glue:hadoop-catalog:v3.notes: Hadoop catalog supported via Glue ETL. Glue: Can use Hadoop catalog via custom Spark configuration on Glue 5.0+; not recommended
caveats: Requires custom Spark configuration
No atomic rename guarantee on S3; Glue Data Catalog recommended instead

aws-glue:jdbc-catalog:v2.notes: JDBC catalog supported via Glue ETL. Glue: Can use JDBC catalog via custom Spark configuration on Glue
caveats: Requires custom Spark configuration, JDBC driver, and endpoint

aws-glue:jdbc-catalog:v3.notes: JDBC catalog supported via Glue ETL. Glue: Can use JDBC catalog via custom Spark configuration on Glue 5.0+
caveats: Requires custom Spark configuration, JDBC driver, and endpoint

aws-glue:table-creation:v2.notes: Create Iceberg tables via Spark SQL DDL (CREATE TABLE, CTAS) or Glue Data Catalog CreateTable API on Glue 3.0+
caveats: Glue Data Catalog UpdateTable API does not support Iceberg table format as input

aws-glue:table-creation:v3.notes: Create V3 tables via Spark SQL DDL with format-version=3 on Glue 5.0+; upgrade V2→V3 via ALTER TABLE SET TBLPROPERTIES
caveats: Requires Glue 5.0+ for V3 format support
V2→V3 upgrade is one-way; cannot downgrade back to V2

aws-glue:variant-type:v2.notes: Variant type is a V3 feature, not available in format version 2

aws-glue:variant-type:v3.notes: Variant type supported via Spark on AWS EMR with Iceberg V3. Glue: Variant type introduced in Iceberg V3; basic support available on Glue 5.1 (Iceberg 1.10.0) but the spec is still evolving
caveats: Requires Glue 5.1 (Iceberg 1.10.0)
Variant type support is still maturing in the Iceberg ecosystem

aws-glue:shredded-variant:v2.notes: Shredded variant is a V3 feature, not available in format version 2

aws-glue:shredded-variant:v3.notes: Shredded variant support in AWS Glue is not yet documented; depends on Iceberg library support
caveats: Not yet documented for AWS Glue

aws-glue:geometry-type:v2.notes: Geometry type is a V3 feature, not available in format version 2

aws-glue:geometry-type:v3.notes: Geometry type support in AWS Glue is not yet documented
caveats: Not yet documented for AWS Glue

aws-glue:lineage:v2.notes: Row lineage is a V3 feature, not available in format version 2

aws-glue:lineage:v3.notes: V3 row lineage provides _row_id and _last_updated_sequence_number metadata fields for precise change tracking; requires Glue 5.1 (Iceberg 1.10.0). EMR: AWS EMR V3 lineage support is not yet documented
caveats: Requires Glue 5.1 (Iceberg 1.10.0)
No historical backfill after V2→V3 upgrade

aws-glue:statistics:v2.notes: AWS Glue writes column statistics to Iceberg manifest files via Spark on Glue 3.0+. Statistics include min/max values, null counts, and value counts. Configurable via write.metadata.metrics.default and per-column settings.

aws-glue:statistics:v3.notes: Column statistics fully supported via Spark on Glue 5.0+ for V3 tables
caveats: Requires Glue 5.0+ for V3 format support

aws-glue:bloom-filters:v2.notes: Full bloom filter support via Glue ETL. Parquet-level bloom filters configurable via write.parquet.bloom-filter-enabled.column.* table properties. Also supports compute_table_stats procedure for Puffin-based statistics.. Glue: Parquet-level bloom filters can be enabled via Iceberg table properties (write.parquet.bloom-filter-enabled.column.*) when writing with Spark on Glue 4.0+. Not enabled by default.
caveats: Requires setting bloom filter table properties per column
Not enabled by default
No Puffin-based bloom filter support

aws-glue:bloom-filters:v3.notes: Full bloom filter support via Glue ETL with Iceberg V3. Glue: Parquet-level bloom filters supported via Iceberg table properties on Glue 5.0+ for V3 tables
caveats: Requires setting bloom filter table properties per column
Requires Glue 5.0+ for V3 format support

aws-glue:type-promotion:v2.notes: AWS Glue supports type promotion via Spark SQL ALTER TABLE ALTER COLUMN TYPE on Iceberg tables. Supports int to bigint, float to double, and decimal precision widening.
caveats: Requires Spark SQL within Glue ETL jobs
Metadata-only operation; no data file rewrite required

aws-glue:type-promotion:v3.notes: Type promotion supported via Spark SQL on Glue 5.0+ for V3 Iceberg tables.

aws-glue:snowflake-horizon-catalog:v2.notes: AWS Glue Spark jobs can connect to Snowflake Horizon Catalog as an external REST catalog. Read access is supported. Write access to externally-managed tables is not supported — Glue Spark only supports writes to Glue Data Catalog natively. This is a Glue engine limitation and does not apply to OSS Spark.
caveats: Read-only — Glue Spark only supports writes to Glue Data Catalog natively
Write limitation is a Glue engine limitation, not a Horizon Catalog limitation
Requires bundling Iceberg REST catalog dependency in Glue job

aws-glue:snowflake-horizon-catalog:v3.notes: V3 support via Glue Spark depends on Iceberg library version used; REST catalog connectivity pattern unchanged.
caveats: V3 support depends on Iceberg library version bundled in Glue job

aws-glue:deletion-vectors:v2.notes: Deletion vectors are a V3 feature; not applicable to Iceberg V2

aws-glue:deletion-vectors:v3.notes: Glue 5.1 (Iceberg 1.10.0) supports Iceberg V3 deletion vectors (Puffin files) for efficient row-level deletes without rewriting data files.
